daily_brief: The dominant story continues to be the US-Israeli war with Iran, now entering its fourth week, with President Trump claiming negotiations are underway while Tehran denies formal talks [BBC, France 24, Le Monde]. Iran's partial reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' vessels prompted a 6% drop in oil prices, though the conflict continues to devastate Global South economies from Pakistan to Egypt [Al Jazeera, Financial Times]. Russia launched its largest aerial attack on Ukraine since the war began, firing 948 drones in 24 hours and killing at least eight people, striking a UNESCO World Heritage site in Lviv and a maternity hospital in Ivano-Frankivsk [BBC]. Denmark's election produced an inconclusive result, with Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats posting their worst showing in over a century despite her prominent stance against Trump's Greenland ambitions [BBC, France 24, The Hindu]. The EU and Australia finalized a landmark free trade deal worth A$10 billion after eight years of negotiations, as Brussels continues diversifying trade relationships amid global uncertainty [BBC, RFI]. Meanwhile, US investigators revealed a controller cleared a fire truck to cross LaGuardia Airport's runway just 12 seconds before an Air Canada jet touched down, killing both pilots [ABC Australia, Globe and Mail].

top_stories.headline: Trump Claims Iran Negotiations Underway, Tehran Opens Hormuz to 'Non-Hostile' Vessels
bullets: Trump announced he sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran and claimed negotiations are happening, though Iran has not confirmed formal talks and a military spokesperson said the US is 'negotiating with itself'
Iran announced 'non-hostile vessels' may transit the Strait of Hormuz if they meet safety and security regulations, prompting Brent crude to drop nearly 6%
At least 1,000 additional US troops from the 82nd Airborne Division are being deployed to the Middle East in coming days
Israel and Iran continue exchanging strikes, with Israeli attacks on Tehran infrastructure and Iranian missiles and drones targeting Israel, US bases in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes international law concerns and quotes former ICC prosecutor calling the war a 'crime of aggression'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focuses on diplomatic developments and Trump's optimistic framing

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Neutral diplomatic focus with attention to tentative nature of claims

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlights economic impact on Iran's population and Global South countries

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Notes Iran's defiant statements and challenges to any potential deal

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes regional impacts including India-Sri Lanka energy discussions

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Largest Aerial Attack of Ukraine War: 948 Drones in 24 Hours
bullets: Ukraine's Air Force reported 948 drones launched over 24 hours, including an unusual daytime wave of 556 drones starting Tuesday morning
At least eight people killed across the attacks; the 16th century Bernardine monastery in Lviv, a UNESCO World Heritage site, was damaged
A maternity hospital was hit in the Ivano-Frankivsk region; 32 people injured in Lviv alone
President Zelensky said the scale 'clearly shows that Russia has no intention of really ending this war'; Russia's military has not commented

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Straightforward factual reporting with emphasis on scale and civilian infrastructure damage

top_stories.headline: Denmark Election Inconclusive as Frederiksen's Social Democrats Post Century-Low Result
bullets: Social Democrats won 21.9% of the vote and 38 seats, their lowest result since 1903, down from 27.5% in 2022
Left-wing bloc secured 84 seats versus 77 for the right, both short of the 90-seat majority needed
Centrist Moderate party with 14 seats becomes kingmaker; Foreign Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen called on rivals to 'come and play with us'
Despite Frederiksen's high-profile defense against Trump's Greenland annexation threats, domestic concerns over migration and cost of living dominated voter priorities

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes Frederiksen's Greenland stance and coalition complexity

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Frames result as 'setback' and 'weakened' position for Frederiksen

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Highlights historical significance of worst result in 120+ years

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focuses on her international standing and relationship with Carney

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes inconclusive nature and global instability context

top_stories.headline: Australia and EU Finalize A$10 Billion Free Trade Deal After Eight Years
bullets: Deal removes almost all tariffs on trade and includes cooperation on defense and critical minerals
Australian beef exports to EU will increase more than tenfold over the next decade, though farmers on both sides expressed dissatisfaction with quotas
European carmakers benefit from Australia raising luxury car tax threshold on electric vehicles; Australian winemakers can use 'prosecco' domestically but must stop for exports after 10 years
European Commission President von der Leyen described the deal as focused on 'collective resilience' in a 'deeply changing' world

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes mutual benefit and geopolitical context of diversifying from US unpredictability

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Details specific compromises on geographical names and wine terms

top_stories.headline: LaGuardia Crash Investigation: Controller Cleared Fire Truck 12 Seconds Before Landing
bullets: NTSB revealed only two air traffic controllers were on duty overnight when a controller cleared the fire truck to cross the runway
The Air Canada jet was traveling approximately 167 km/h when it struck the Port Authority emergency vehicle
Both pilots were killed; 39 people hospitalized with six still in hospital as of reporting
Airport's ground surveillance system did not generate an alert, and the fire truck lacked a transponder to transmit its location to air traffic control

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Focuses on systemic failures and multiple safety layers that failed

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes pilots had 'little chance of survival' and includes Canadian angle on Montreal-origin flight

additional_stories.headline: OpenAI shuts down Sora video-generation app and ends Disney partnership to focus on robotics and agentic AI
BlackRock CEO Larry Fink warns oil at $150 per barrel would trigger global recession
Strike on Sudan hospital kills at least 64 people including children, WHO reports
Two Russians face trial in Angola on charges of attempting to interfere in elections on behalf of Wagner-linked network
Australia and France warn Israel against planned occupation of southern Lebanon up to Litani River
Brazil's Supreme Court grants former President Bolsonaro 90-day house arrest on humanitarian grounds after hospitalization
New Mexico jury orders Meta to pay $375 million for knowingly harming children's mental health
TotalEnergies signs deal with US to redirect $928 million from offshore wind projects to natural gas production
Second set of rare mountain gorilla twins born in DRC's Virunga National Park within two months
Thousands march in Buenos Aires to mark 50th anniversary of Argentina's military coup and honor 30,000 disappeared
